description:
You are an edge-computing engineer who is preparing a deployment package for a fleet of IoT gateways.  
Everything you need is already under  

/home/user/edge_project/

1. Source data  
   • /home/user/edge_project/devices_master.tsv – a 7-column, tab-separated file whose first line is a header.  
     Header columns (in order):  
       1. device_id  
       2. model  
       3. firmware_version  
       4. ip_address  
       5. uptime_hours  
       6. city  
       7. ready_for_deploy  

2. Your goal  
   a. Build a new sub-directory:  
      /home/user/edge_project/deploy_batch/  

   b. Inside that directory create EXACTLY the three files listed below, each with the precise format shown.  
      • deploy_list.tsv   – a tab-separated list of only those devices whose ready_for_deploy column contains “yes”.  
        It must keep the original record order from devices_master.tsv, include the header line shown below, and contain precisely the 3 columns listed, in this order:  
            device_id    ip_address    city  
        Example of required final content (tabs between columns, no extra spaces):  
            device_id	ip_address	city  
            gw-001	192.168.0.11	NewYork  
            gw-003	192.168.0.30	NewYork  
            gw-004	172.16.1.10	Tokyo  
            gw-005	10.0.0.60	Berlin  

      • count_by_city.tsv – a two-column, tab-separated summary derived ONLY from deploy_list.tsv.  
        It must be sorted by city name alphabetically (case-sensitive), include the header line below, and have one line per distinct city:  
            city	ready_device_count  
            Berlin	1  
            NewYork	2  
            Tokyo	1  

      • command_log.txt – a plain-text, chronological list of every shell command you intentionally executed to accomplish parts (a) and (b).  
        One command per line, no line numbers, no timestamps, no comments.  
        It will be used only for validation of approach; therefore do not remove or edit it after you are finished.  

3. Restrictions & hints  
   • Use standard GNU coreutils available in a typical Linux shell (e.g., cut, paste, grep, sort, uniq, etc.).  
   • Do NOT manually type the data shown above; everything must be produced programmatically from devices_master.tsv.  
   • The automated checker will fail if:  
       – The directory or any files are missing or mis-named.  
       – Files contain carriage returns, extra blank lines, additional spaces, different column order, or different sorting.  
       – Tabs are replaced with spaces (the test harness checks byte-for-byte).  

4. Verification expectations  
   After you finish, an automated script will perform these exact checks:  
     • test -d /home/user/edge_project/deploy_batch  
     • test -f /home/user/edge_project/deploy_batch/deploy_list.tsv  
     • test -f /home/user/edge_project/deploy_batch/count_by_city.tsv  
     • test -f /home/user/edge_project/deploy_batch/command_log.txt  
     • diff – The two TSV files must exactly match the reference byte sequence, including tab characters and newlines.  
     • command_log.txt must contain at least 5 and at most 10 non-empty lines.  

Your task: use the terminal to construct the deploy_batch directory and populate the three files so that all verification steps succeed.  
The entire solution can reasonably be achieved with 5-10 well-crafted shell commands.
truth:
Initial file present when the task starts:

Path: /home/user/edge_project/devices_master.tsv  
Contents (each “→” represents a literal tab character, but the file actually contains tabs, not the arrows):

device_id→model→firmware_version→ip_address→uptime_hours→city→ready_for_deploy
gw-001→A1→1.0.3→192.168.0.11→240→NewYork→yes
gw-002→B2→1.0.1→10.0.0.50→12→Berlin→no
gw-003→A1→1.0.4→192.168.0.30→48→NewYork→yes
gw-004→C3→1.2.0→172.16.1.10→5→Tokyo→yes
gw-005→B2→1.0.1→10.0.0.60→96→Berlin→yes

Expected files after the agent completes:

1. /home/user/edge_project/deploy_batch/deploy_list.tsv  
(device_id, ip_address, city; tab-separated; Unix line endings)

device_id→ip_address→city
gw-001→192.168.0.11→NewYork
gw-003→192.168.0.30→NewYork
gw-004→172.16.1.10→Tokyo
gw-005→10.0.0.60→Berlin


2. /home/user/edge_project/deploy_batch/count_by_city.tsv  
(city, ready_device_count; tab-separated; sorted alphabetically by city)

city→ready_device_count
Berlin→1
NewYork→2
Tokyo→1


3. /home/user/edge_project/deploy_batch/command_log.txt  
Contains between 5 and 10 non-empty lines (the specific commands are not enforced, only line count).  

Permissions:  
The /home/user directory and everything under it are owned by user:user, default umask 022; the agent has full write permission inside /home/user.

The automated test will compare deploy_list.tsv and count_by_city.tsv byte-for-byte against the content shown above (with real tab and newline characters).
